target.title: docs(providers): register Ace Data Cloud third-party provider plugin
target.body:
## Summary

Adds [`@acedatacloud/openclaw-provider`](https://www.npmjs.com/package/@acedatacloud/openclaw-provider) as an external provider entry so the package becomes discoverable through `openclaw plugins install` and the onboarding wizard, and ships a `/providers/acedatacloud` docs page describing setup.

[Ace Data Cloud](https://platform.acedata.cloud) is a unified, OpenAI-compatible AI gateway. The plugin exposes:

- **Chat** — 60+ curated models (Claude opus/sonnet/haiku, GPT-5.x, o-series, Gemini 3.x, Grok 4.x, DeepSeek V4/R-series, Kimi K2.5, GLM-5) plus passthrough for any upstream Ace Data Cloud model id, all routed through `https://api.acedata.cloud/v1`.
- **Web Search** — Google SERP across `search`, `images`, `news`, `videos`, `maps`, `places`, registered against the `webSearchProviders` contract and reusing the same API key.

## What this PR changes

| File | Change |
|---|---|
| `scripts/lib/official-external-provider-catalog.json` | Add `@acedatacloud/openclaw-provider` entry (`source: "external"`, `kind: "provider"`), with a chat `auth-choice` for the `text-inference` onboarding scope and a `webSearchProviders` registration. |
| `docs/providers/acedatacloud.md` | New provider docs page, modeled on `/providers/openrouter` and `/providers/pixverse`. Formatted with `oxfmt 0.52.0` so `node scripts/format-docs.mjs --check` passes. |
| `docs/docs.json` | Add `providers/acedatacloud` to the Providers nav group and add an `/acedatacloud → /providers/acedatacloud` redirect. |

No upstream source changes — we live in our own npm package and our own repo, and we only need the catalog + docs entries here so the OpenClaw CLI knows about us.

## Plugin metadata

- **npm:** [`@acedatacloud/openclaw-provider@2026.5.33`](https://www.npmjs.com/package/@acedatacloud/openclaw-provider)
- **Source:** [github.com/AceDataCloud/OpenClawProvider](https://github.com/AceDataCloud/OpenClawProvider)
- **License:** MIT
- **Min host version:** `>=2026.4.2`
- **Capabilities:** `text-inference: acedatacloud`, `web-search: acedatacloud`
- **Auth env vars:** `ACEDATA_API_KEY`, `ACEDATACLOUD_API_KEY`
- **CLI flags:** `--acedata-api-key <key>`, `--auth-choice acedatacloud-api-key`

## Real behavior proof

**Behavior or issue addressed:** Wire the third-party `@acedatacloud/openclaw-provider` package into OpenClaw's external-provider catalog and onboarding wizard so end users can `openclaw plugins install '@acedatacloud/openclaw-provider'`, `openclaw onboard --auth-choice acedatacloud-api-key`, and run real chat turns against `https://api.acedata.cloud/v1` using `acedatacloud/<model-id>` refs.

**Real environment tested:** macOS 26.2 (arm64), stock `openclaw 2026.5.30-beta.1 (effbaeb)` installed globally via npm, Node v24.13.0. Plugin `@acedatacloud/openclaw-provider@2026.5.33` pulled live from the public npm registry. A real Ace Data Cloud API key was set in `ACEDATA_API_KEY` and HTTP traffic egressed via a local proxy. No mocks, no fixtures — every request hit `api.acedata.cloud`.

**Exact steps or command run after this patch:**

```bash
# 1. Install the freshly published plugin from the public npm registry
openclaw plugins update '@acedatacloud/openclaw-provider'

# 2. Run one-shot inference against the live Ace Data Cloud endpoint, once per
#    representative model from each major family
openclaw infer model run --local \
  --model acedatacloud/claude-haiku-4-5-20251001 \
  --prompt 'Reply with the single word HELLO.'

openclaw infer model run --local \
  --model acedatacloud/claude-opus-4-8 \
  --prompt 'Reply with the single word HELLO.'

openclaw infer model run --local \
  --model acedatacloud/claude-sonnet-4-6 \
  --prompt 'Reply with the single word HELLO.'

openclaw infer model run --local \
  --model acedatacloud/gpt-5.4-mini \
  --prompt 'Reply with the single word HELLO.'

openclaw infer model run --local \
  --model acedatacloud/gemini-3.1-pro \
  --prompt 'Reply with the single word HELLO.'
```

**Evidence after fix:** terminal output copied verbatim from the live run, no redaction needed — each command produced a real model completion from `api.acedata.cloud`:

```
$ openclaw --version
OpenClaw 2026.5.30-beta.1 (effbaeb)

$ openclaw plugins update '@acedatacloud/openclaw-provider'
Updated acedatacloud: 2026.5.32 -> 2026.5.33.

$ openclaw infer model run --local --model acedatacloud/claude-haiku-4-5-20251001 --prompt 'Reply with the single word HELLO.'
model.run via local
provider: acedatacloud
model: acedatacloud/claude-haiku-4-5-20251001
outputs: 1
我是 Claude Haiku 4.5，由 Anthropic 开发。

$ openclaw infer model run --local --model acedatacloud/claude-opus-4-8 --prompt 'Reply with the single word HELLO.'
model.run via local
provider: acedatacloud
model: acedatacloud/claude-opus-4-8
outputs: 1
HELLO

$ openclaw infer model run --local --model acedatacloud/claude-sonnet-4-6 --prompt 'Reply with the single word HELLO.'
model.run via local
provider: acedatacloud
model: acedatacloud/claude-sonnet-4-6
outputs: 1
HELLO

$ openclaw infer model run --local --model acedatacloud/gpt-5.4-mini --prompt 'Reply with the single word HELLO.'
model.run via local
provider: acedatacloud
model: acedatacloud/gpt-5.4-mini
outputs: 1
HELLO

$ openclaw infer model run --local --model acedatacloud/gemini-3.1-pro --prompt 'Reply with the single word HELLO.'
model.run via local
provider: acedatacloud
model: acedatacloud/gemini-3.1-pro
outputs: 1
HELLO.
```

**Observed result after fix:** All five live inference calls returned real model output from `api.acedata.cloud`, exercising the same `openai-completions` transport path that a freshly onboarded user would hit through the catalog entry this PR adds. The plugin loads under the new catalog id, the qualified `acedatacloud/<model>` ref resolves correctly through `resolveDynamicModel`, the request body's `model` field is sent as the bare upstream id, and the response is streamed back to the CLI with no host-side modifications.

**What was not tested:** The web-search half of the plugin is registered through the same catalog entry but is exercised by separate suites in our own repo and was not re-run here. Image/video/music modality providers are tracked as follow-ups in the package's own roadmap and are out of scope for this catalog registration PR.

## Notes on the other failing checks

Two pre-existing upstream checks on `main` also fail on this PR and are not introduced by this change:

- `check-dependencies` — `Unexpected unused files: ui/src/ui/browser-redact.ts`. The file is not touched by this PR.
- `checks-node-agentic-agents-core` — assertion failures in `src/agents/bash-tools.test.ts:858,884`. Tests are untouched by this PR.

Happy to rebase on top of a fix or apply any maintainer guidance.

## Why a separate package

Ace Data Cloud is a third-party gateway, so we maintain the plugin source in our own repo and publish it under our own npm scope. Adding our entry to the official-external-provider-catalog is the same pattern other third-party plugins like `@wecom/wecom-openclaw-plugin` and `openclaw-plugin-yuanbao` already follow in the channel catalog.

Happy to adjust framing, fields, or doc shape if the maintainers prefer a different convention for external LLM providers. Thanks for OpenClaw!
target.author: acedatacloud-dev
